title: podman-search: run in parallel
body:
* Run image searches in parallel.
* Refactor code to be used by podman and the varlink API.
* Extend varlink API with an image search filter.

Fixes: #2345

Does this actually fix the original issue? It sounded like they wanted
results streamed, as opposed to Varlink waiting for the entire process to
finish before returning.

Regardless, this is a good change - just maybe not the exact one they asked
for.

> Does this actually fix the original issue? It sounded like they wanted results streamed, as opposed to Varlink waiting for the entire process to finish before returning. Regardless, this is a good change - just maybe not the exact one they asked for.

That's a valid point. To preserve the order (and to avoid a random sequence) the "printing as soon as finished" would need to happen according to the order of the registries. I am not sure that's worth the effort excluding cases where a few queries take unusually long.
Oh, wait. I missed the Varlink API point entirely. Thanks, @mheon!
To make this right, I need to refactor most parts into `libpod/image`. I am on it :)
@mheon could you have another look. The code is now refactored, the Varlink API makes use of it and is extended with a filter. @jwhonce might be interested in this PR as well :)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2348) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Changes LGTM but would like a nod from @baude on the Varlink stuff

Changes LGTM, but it looks like you're fighting some test unhappiness.

```
[Fail] Podman top [It] podman pod top on pod with containers in different namespace 
/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/pod_top_test.go:133

[Fail] Podman checkpoint [It] podman checkpoint container with established tcp connections 
/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/checkpoint_test.go:207
```

Tests are green now. Looked like a hiccup in the CI.

Now we need @baude's Varlink expertise :)
We really need the cockpit people to test this, but it LGTM.

> We really need the cockpit people to test this, but it LGTM.

We've tested via the Varlink CL. Currently, the PR does everything but to stream the results when they're available. I don't think that's necessary anymore due to the parallel execution. It also avoids complex code.
